data.title: THE DOCUMENTS FOLDER MISTAKE.
blocks: A project manager at a nonprofit needs a briefing document. She has been working on this program for two years. Her lapt
The Documents Folder Mistake — She opens Cowork, grants access to her Documents folder — it is where all the program files are — and asks for a briefin

S03.meta: // SLIDE 03 — WHAT A SAFE WORKSPACE IS

data.title: WHAT A SAFE WORKSPACE IS.
blocks: · Specifies what Cowork may read (input files)
· Specifies where Cowork may write (output folder)
· Separates the task files from everything else on your machine
· Preserves originals so the task can be reversed
· Documents what the task is and what Cowork is allowed to do

data.title: PREPARING THE WORKSPACE: STEP BY STEP
blocks: · Names, phone numbers, addresses, or identifying details that are not necessary for the task
· Private comments, editorial notes, or side conversations embedded in documents
· Credentials, passwords, or access tokens in any format
· Anything you are not authorized to share with an AI tool under your organization's policies
· What this workspace is for

S06.meta: // SLIDE 06 — WHAT COWORK CAN AND CANNOT ACC

data.title: WHAT COWORK CAN AND CANNOT ACCESS
blocks: Cowork's access to your computer is not automatic. You grant it by connecting local folders or enabling connectors . The
What Cowork Can and Cannot Access — When you grant access to a folder, Cowork can see the contents of that folder. If you grant a broad folder — Documents,
data.section: WHAT COWORK CAN AND CANNOT ACC

S07.meta: // SLIDE 07 — COMPUTER USE AND THE VISIBLE D

data.title: COMPUTER USE AND THE VISIBLE DESKTOP
blocks: · Close tabs and applications not relevant to the task before starting
· Log out of sensitive portals you do not need open
· Use a clean browser profile or context where possible
· Treat "visible on screen" as "accessible to Cowork" in your planning
data.section: COMPUTER USE AND THE VISIBLE D

data.title: THE BACKUP REQUIREMENT.
blocks: Any task that involves renaming, reformatting, cleaning, or transforming files requires a backup of the originals before
The Backup Requirement — Cowork may be asked to reorganize a folder, rename files according to a convention, clean up a spreadsheet, or reformat

data.title: THE HUMAN GATE IN THIS CHAPTER.
blocks: Workspace preparation is entirely human work. Cowork cannot decide what it should be allowed to see. That judgment — thi
The Human Gate in This Chapter — The data minimization principle from NIST's Privacy Framework is a practical guide: provide only the information necessa
data.section: THE HUMAN GATE IN THIS CHAPTER

data.title: COMMON MISTAKES
blocks: "If the file is on my computer, it is okay to share."
"A folder boundary is only for neatness."
"Copies are unnecessary if Cowork is careful."
"Sensitive data means only passwords."
"Deleting sensitive files after the output fixes the problem."

data.title: THE CORE CLAIM.
